name: Mustard Chicken Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine sugar, flour, ginger, salt, water, mustard, reduced-sodium soy sauce, rice-wine vinegar, and garlic. Mix well. Add chicken halves and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
2. Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Using a slotted spoon, remove the chicken from the marinade and add it to the skillet. Reserve the marinade. Stir-fry the chicken for 3 minutes, or until cooked through. Remove the chicken to a plate to keep warm. Wipe out the skillet with paper towels.
3. Heat the remaining tablespoon of vegetable oil in the same skillet over medium heat. Add green onions and stir-fry for 1 minute. Add snow peas and red pepper, stirring for another 3 minutes. Pour in the reserved marinade. Bring to a boil. Return the chicken to the skillet and cook, stirring, for an additional 2 minutes, or until the sauce is bubbly and thickened.
